title: Systemic description.json lastUpdated staleness across every phase folder in 026-graph-and-context-optimization/ — 16/16 folders are stale or missing the field
evidence: Full-tree sweep: 16/16 sibling phase folders examined. 11 folders (001-006, 011-014, 016) have description.lastUpdated strictly older than graph.derived.last_save_at. 4 folders (007, 008, 009, 010) have no lastUpdated field at all. 1 folder (015) has lastUpdated but no graph last_save_at. FRESH count: 0/16. Worst drift: 001-003 are ≥2 days stale. Active drift: 016 at 14 hours. Empirically proves R4-P1-002's infrastructure-gap prediction has materialized as tree-wide invariant violation.
impact: Memory-search consumers reading description.json as authority for current packet state see stale picture for every 026-tree packet. Skill-advisor graph traversal uses lastUpdated for recency-ranking — degraded across the entire tree. Not a one-packet issue (R3-P2-001) but a systemic failure pattern.
recommendation: (a) Amend generate-context.js to write description.json.lastUpdated during canonical saves. (b) Re-run canonical save across all 16 folders to backfill. (c) Add CI check that description.lastUpdated ≥ graph.last_save_at.

claimAdjudication.counterevidenceSought: Checked whether 4 missing-field folders (007-010) are archived/legacy — their graph timestamps are current-generation (2026-04-13), not archived. Checked whether stale folders are intentionally frozen — git log shows active code changes recently. Checked whether lastUpdated is declared non-authoritative — no documentation says this; system-spec-kit SKILL.md cites both as canonical.
claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: Team may treat lastUpdated as a reference timestamp; but field naming, inconsistent presence, and SKILL.md citing both as canonical contradict any intentional policy.
claimAdjudication.downgradeTrigger: generate-context.js amended to write lastUpdated + sweep re-runs to achieve 16/16 fresh; OR CLAUDE.md amended to declare lastUpdated non-authoritative

title: p0-a-cross-runtime-tempdir-poisoning.vitest.ts OpenCode coverage is purely by-extension because no OpenCode hook module exists to import
evidence: Test name at line 67 promises 'Claude, Gemini, and OpenCode consumers'. Lines 9 and 11 import only handleClaudeStartup and handleGeminiCompact. Glob for hooks/opencode/*.ts returns zero files. OpenCode coverage is structural-by-schema-share (OpenCode uses same tmpdir path schema as Claude/Gemini) but zero OpenCode-specific code is exercised. Refines R3-P2-003 with concrete structural finding.
impact: If OpenCode ever grows its own hooks/opencode/session-prime.ts, the test has zero coverage against it by construction — the name promise cannot be honored because the target module does not exist.
recommendation: (a) Rename test to 'Claude and Gemini' + header comment explaining OpenCode inherits coverage by schema-share, OR (b) create a minimal hooks/opencode/session-prime.ts stub so the assertion is literal.

claimAdjudication.counterevidenceSought: Searched for any hooks/opencode/*.ts — none. Searched for OpenCode-specific handleStartup or handleCompact in shared/ — none. Searched phase-017 commit log for opencode/session-prime — no commits match.
claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: Team may have decided OpenCode uses Claude path directly (shared runtime); if documented, renaming would suffice; no such doc exists in CLAUDE.md, system-spec-kit SKILL.md, or closing-pass-notes.md.
claimAdjudication.downgradeTrigger: Test renamed + comment added; or OpenCode hook stub created and asserted explicitly

crossRefs: R3-P2-003 (p0-a test name overclaim, original observation)

name: Scope-normalization drift
rootCause: canonical normalizeScopeContext under-adopted; 4 disconnected reinvented normalizers
currentExposure: none — iter 5 verified normalizers semantically equivalent
futureExposure: latent — future author modifying one copy without others re-introduces R1-P1-001 drift
recommendedAction: collapse 4 reinvented to imports of normalizeScopeContext; classify as P1

name: Canonical-save-surface hygiene
rootCause: canonical-save pipeline is not self-healing; generate-context.js never writes description.json.lastUpdated; no git hooks; no lint for evidence markers; manual-dispatch bypasses pipeline
currentExposure: severe and measurable — 0/16 folders fresh; research folder missing both required files; 95% malformed evidence markers
recommendedAction: single synthesized P1 remediation: (a) generate-context.js writes lastUpdated; (b) packet-metadata backfill for research folders; (c) evidence-marker lint; (d) re-run canonical save

name: ASCII-only sanitization
rootCause: multiple Phase 017 string-matching surfaces share ASCII-only assumption without a shared character-set contract; no NFKC applied
currentExposure: bounded — same-UID homoglyph injection requires attacker-writable hook-state (0o600 perms); OpenCode cross-runtime coverage is schema-share-only
recommendedAction: document ASCII-only character-set contract explicitly; or introduce NFKC normalization layer

coverage: Claude + Gemini explicit; OpenCode structural-by-schema-share (no OpenCode hook module exists)
conclusion: Attack chain (tmpdir poisoning → quarantine → cross-runtime recovery) is covered for Claude+Gemini; OpenCode claim is name-only

coverage: 3 independent it() blocks map 1:1 to B1 (CAS conflict_stale_predecessor), B2 (complement window TOCTOU), B3 (batched governed-scope read)
conclusion: Phase coverage accurate; each phase is a distinct test, not a compound

coverage: Full laundering chain: malformed JSON rejection (schema.ts:210-222), schema_version drift (:210-222), legacy line-based fallback (:225-256), preserved-error chaining (:258-273), migrated marker (integration.ts:11,140-169), ranking inversion boost→penalty (integration.ts:140-151 boost; :153-169 penalty)
conclusion: Chain coverage complete; boost and penalty asserted independently

coverage: fs.openSync spy (line 89-95) triggers mid-cleanup saveState call for fresh generation; cleanup.skipped === 1 with reason identity_changed_before_cleanup asserted (:101-108)

hypothesis: session-discovery attack via cross-session summary leak + research metadata missing
evidence: R2-P1-001 affects hook-state tmpdir (per-session); R3-P1-002 affects packet metadata graph. Non-intersecting surfaces.

hypothesis: CAS never actually protects anything (empty-string bypass + 5x normalizer + missing exhaustiveness)
evidence: iter 5 verified normalizeScopeValue and getOptionalString produce equivalent output for all inputs; CAS guard works. Exhaustiveness gap (R4-P2-002) affects typed-union consumers, orthogonal to scope-normalizer.

recommendedNextFocus: Iteration 6: RECOVERY / final sweep — re-check confirmed-clean surfaces, verify no P0 hidden in 5 iterations of 'no new P0', re-examine runPostInsertEnrichment domains for hidden correctness bugs, re-examine executeConflict duplicate transaction branches for drift, decide whether CP-001/CP-003 should upgrade given cumulative evidence, verify boost/penalty deltas match production values, sample remaining 9 focus files (response-builder, opencode-transport, checkpoints, memory-search, skill_graph_compiler, 4 YAMLs).
